title: Haggling for a shirt
desc: Negotiate a price at the market
setting: At the market you spot a shirt you like. The vendor notices your interest.

steps.speaker: Vendedor
steps.es: Esa camisa le queda bien. Doscientos córdobas.
steps.en: That shirt suits you. Two hundred córdobas.

options.es: Está un poco caro. ¿No hay rebaja?
options.en: It's a bit pricey. Any discount?
options.feedback: Textbook haggling — polite, but you opened the negotiation. Expected here.
You can accept, but the first price almost always has room. Try a counter.
If you don't want it, fine — but you said you liked it. Mixed signals.

steps.speaker: Vendedor
steps.es: Para vos, ciento setenta. Es buen precio.
steps.en: For you, one seventy. It's a good price.

options.feedback: A confident counter-offer. Meeting in the middle is the whole game.
Fair enough — you can settle here. But one more nudge often works.
Walking away is a valid tactic — sometimes they call you back with a better price!

steps.speaker: Vendedor
steps.es: Uy, me estás matando. Ciento cincuenta y es tuya.
steps.en: Oof, you're killing me. One fifty and it's yours.

options.feedback: Deal sealed with a perfect 'dale pues.' You haggled like a local.
Clear acceptance — 'la llevo' (I'll take it) is exactly right.
Pushing too hard after a fair drop can kill the deal. Know when to close.

steps.speaker: Vendedor
steps.es: Buena compra. Te la pongo en una bolsa.
steps.en: Good buy. I'll put it in a bag for you.

options.feedback: Friendly close. A good haggle ends with everyone smiling.

options.en: I hope it doesn't fall apart.
options.feedback: A little insulting after he gave you a deal! Better to keep it warm.
